findings.title: New plan doc missing required claims: frontmatter block
findings.why_it_matters: The plan doc is dated 2026-07-06 (post the 2026-05-20 cutoff) but its YAML frontmatter has no `claims:` key at all. Running `plan-check` against the file locally reproduces this: it exits 8 with `PlanClaimsMissingOnPostCutoff`. AGENTS.md states this is enforced by the `plan-claims-gate` workflow on every PR against `main`; once that check is promoted from non-required to required (the repo's own doc says promotion was scheduled for 2026-06-02, which has already passed as of the current date), this file will redden CI and block merge until either a real `claims:` block or the explicit `claims: {}` opt-out is added.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `claims: {}` to the frontmatter (the documented explicit opt-out, appropriate since this is a parked, no-implementation-yet plan) or, if specific paths/SHAs should be tracked, a real `claims:` block per the AGENTS.md example — then re-run `plan-check docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-feat-error-dashboard-stateblock-capture-plan.md` to confirm exit 0.
evidence: AGENTS.md: 'Any plan-doc dated 2026-05-20 or later must include a claims: block (or explicit claims: {} opt-out) — otherwise plan-check exits 8 and the plan-claims-gate check fails.'

residual_risks: The new '.pending' (never-published) banner state is, by the author's own commit-message admission, never captured by the /api/v1/error-reports dashboard (all 5 capture hooks are exception-based; a gracefully-rendered banner doesn't fire any of them). This is tracked in the newly parked docs/plans/2026-07-06-001-feat-error-dashboard-stateblock-capture-plan.md rather than fixed here, which is consistent with the stated decision to keep this fix presentation-only — not a standards violation, just a known follow-up dependency worth surfacing.

The CSS/JS change is fully compliant with webui_app/AGENTS.md and root CLAUDE.md's 'Frontend anti-rot' rules for the legacy Jinja layer: no inline on* handlers, no window.* globals introduced, only textContent (no innerHTML) used for the new copy, and the new color uses the existing var(--info-soft) token rather than a raw literal (confirmed tests/test_webui_css_no_raw_colors.py still passes, 5/5 green, so the stated reasoning that this avoids inflating the index.css raw-literal ceiling holds).
testing_gaps: No automated test (JS or Python) exercises _initHealthBar's new `neverPublished` branch (reasons.length === 1 && reasons[0] === 'pipeline:never_run' vs. any other degraded combination). This matches AGENTS.md's own stated convention that legacy `static/js/` page-level interaction has no test framework and is verified only by manual browser walkthrough — so it is not a standards violation, but it is a real coverage gap the commit message itself discloses ('legacy static/js/ 沒有自動化前端測試框架, 此修復僅有瀏覽器手動驗證, 無回歸測試').
